=:
name on the left, stuff on the right
put it in the box, now it is in sight
==:
double equals, time to compare
same or not, we check it there
!=:
not the same, that is the claim
if they differ, that is the game
>:
bigger than, left beats right
larger wins this little fight
<:
smaller than, left is less
that is the side that wins the test
>=:
greater or equal, win or tie
left side passes, do not ask why
<=:
less or equal, small or same
left side passes in the game
(: open the door, take a look inside
): close it up, nothing left to hide
[:
open a box, hold a few
many things inside for you
]: close the box, we are done with you
{:
open a map, keys and things
pairs of values that it brings
}:
close the map, that is the end
no more pairs to recommend
::
colon says do this next
get ready now, here comes the text
,:
comma says not done yet
there is more to come, do not forget
dot means go inside the thing
reach its parts and what they bring
#:
hashtag note, the code cannot see
this is just a note for me
indent:
step to the right, you are inside
step back left, now you are outside

general:
name the box, drop it in
that is how all your values begin
number:
just a count, plain and true
one, two, three, it is up to you
float:
numbers with dots, split in two
whole and part both come through
string:
quotes mean words, not math to do
just some text we pass right through
boolean:
true or false, yes or no
that is the way decisions go
loop_variable:
little box that changes fast
new number each time we pass

print:
what is inside, comes outside
say it to the screen with pride
def:
build a machine, give it a name
run it later, same each time
call:
call the machine, press go
watch it do the thing you know
return:
send it back, do not keep it
give the answer, now we are through
input:
ask the user, wait and see
what they type comes back to me
len:
count them up, how many there
length is what we want to share
type:
what is this, what is its kind
tell me what this thing is defined

for:
say for once, do it more
same thing again, like before
in:
pick from inside, take your turn
grab each piece as we return
range:
start at zero, climb the ladder
stop before n, not a step after
while:
while it is true, keep the ride
stop when false, step outside
break:
stop it now, we are done for real
jump right out, that is the deal
continue:
skip this turn, do not stay long
jump ahead and move along

if:
if it is true, walk on through
if it is not, we are done with you
else:
if that failed, here is the deal
do this part instead for real
elif:
try again, one more test
maybe this one works out best

brackets:
open a box that holds a few
many things inside for you
items:
one by one, in a row
that is how lists begin to grow
index:
start at zero, that is the key
first thing lives at zero, see
append:
add one more onto the end
make the list a growing friend
pop:
take one off, from the end
shorter list is what we send

general:
keys and values, pair them tight
look them up to get it right
key_access:
use the key to find the prize
open the box and see what lies

+: add it up, combine the pile
*: times it out, grow it wide
/: split it up, share the ride
%:
what remains after divide
that is the part we keep inside
**:
power up, raise it high
multiply again and fly
and: both must pass, yes and yes
or: one is enough, pass the test
not: flip it around, true to false

int: turn to number, drop the text
str: turn to words, show it next
float:
add a dot, make it split
now it is not a whole

import:
bring it in from far away
use it here inside your play
from:
pick a part from something big
just one piece is what you dig
as:
give it a new name to use
short or simple, you can choose

for_loop_full:
start at zero, climb the ladder
stop before the final number
each step forward, say the name
count it out again again
while_loop_full:
keep on going while it is true
check each time what you should do
once it fails, you step away
loop is done, no more to say
if_else_full:
check the rule, open the door
if it fails, try one more
if none work, last one stays
that is the path the program plays
assignment_full:
name the box, fill it right
now that value is locked in tight
print_full:
take a thing and let it show
send it out so we can know

try:
try it first, take the shot
if it works, great, if not we have got
except:
catch the fall, do not let it crash
handle errors, skip the smash
finally:
no matter what, this will run
last step here when all is done
class:
make a blueprint, shape the thing
many copies this can bring
self:
this one here, the current one
talking about itself when done
yield:
give one piece, then pause the flow
come back later, start it slow
lambda:
tiny function, quick and light
one-line trick, no name in sight

list_comprehension:
build a list, quick and tight
take what fits, leave what is not right
list_comprehension_if:
check each piece as it goes by
keep the ones that pass the eye
nested_comprehension:
loop inside, loop again
build it up from nested runs

nested_for_loops:
loop the outer, step it through
inside that, another loop to do
nested_if:
check one rule, then check again
layered choices now begin
loop_inside_if:
if it passes, start the spin
loop begins once you are in
if_inside_loop:
each step checks before it goes
some get through, some it slows

params_multiple:
many inputs, lined in place
values come to fill each space
default_params:
if you do not give one, that is okay
this default will save the day
return_expression:
do the work, do not delay
send the answer back this way
nested_function:
function inside, hidden tight
only used in here, not outside

method_call:
reach inside, use its skill
call its power, do its will
method_chain:
one step done, then one more
chain them up to do the chore

dict_access:
use the key to find the prize
open the box and see what lies
dict_add:
new key, new value pair
add it in, now it is there
dict_loop:
key and value, walk them through
one by one, like lists do

try_except_full:
try it first, take the chance
if it fails, we change the dance
try_except_else:
if no error came to play
this part runs the normal way
try_except_finally:
no matter what, this will land
final step, just as planned

class_definition:
make a blueprint, shape the thing
many copies this can bring
init:
when it is made, this runs first
sets it up before it bursts
method:
a function tied to what you made
works with data that it saved
self_usage:
this object here, the one in play
holds its data all the way

open:
open the file, take a peek
read or write what you seek
read:
pull it in, from file to you
now the data comes into view
write:
send it out, from you to file
save it there for quite a while
with:
open it safe, close it right
handled for you, neat and tight

list_comprehension_full:
take each thing, test it fast
build a list from what has passed
nested_loop_full:
outer moves, inner runs
many passes, lots gets done
function_with_return_full:
take some in, do the deed
send it back, that is what we need
class_full:
build a mold, make a kind
objects follow that design
file_read_full:
open, read, then close the door
data is yours forevermore

tuple:
packed together, fixed in place
ordered items, locked in space
set:
no repeats, just one each
unique items in your reach
set_add:
add it in, if it is not there
sets keep things unique and fair
tuple_unpack:
split it out, piece by piece
one big thing becomes many pieces

enumerate:
count and item, side by side
index tells where things reside
zip:
walk together, pair the two
first with first all the way through
reversed:
start from end, go back instead
walk it backwards, just as said
sorted:
put in order, small to tall
line them up so sense can call

strip:
trim the edges, cut the ends
clean the start and finish, friends
lower:
make it small, all the way
lowercase is here to stay
upper:
make it loud, letters shout
uppercase is what it is about
replace:
find a piece, swap it out
change the text, no doubt about
split:
break apart into a list
cut by marks that you insist
join:
bring together, piece by piece
merge the list into one piece

round:
near the number, make it neat
round it off to closest seat
abs:
drop the sign, keep the size
distance only, that is the prize
min:
find the smallest in the group
lowest one inside the loop
max:
find the biggest, take the crown
largest value standing tall
sum:
add them all, total score
combine the values into more

pass:
do nothing here, just a mark
placeholder sitting in the dark
global:
reach outside the local space
use the name from a wider place
nonlocal:
not global wide, but not in here
reach the parent that is near

is:
same thing exactly, not just alike
same object, not just type
is_not:
not the same, not that one
pointing elsewhere, different done
in_operator:
is it inside, does it belong
check the group, right or wrong
not_in:
not inside, nowhere found
missing from this list or ground

slice:
take a part, not the whole
cut a section from the roll
slice_start_stop:
start here, stop before
cut the middle, leave the shore
slice_step:
skip along, not every one
step by step till you are done

args:
many inputs, gathered tight
all packed in from left to right
kwargs:
named inputs, key and value
many choices just for you
decorator:
wrap a function, add some flair
change its behavior with extra care

generator_expression:
like a list but slow and light
give one piece, not all in sight
yield_full:
give one now, then wait your turn
come back later, we return

dict_comprehension:
build a map from what you see
keys and values built quickly
set_comprehension:
build a set, keep it neat
no repeats allowed to meet

except_specific:
catch this kind, not all the rest
handle this, ignore the test
raise:
throw an error, stop the show
something is wrong, we need to know

module_call:
reach the tool from where it lives
use the power that it gives
alias_use:
short new name, easier say
same thing works a simpler way

enumerate_loop_full:
count and value, walk in line
index and item both align
zip_loop_full:
pair them up and walk together
step by step through any weather
slice_full:
cut a piece from start to end
just a part, not the blend
generator_full:
one at a time, do not rush the flow
slowly give what we should know
decorator_full:
wrap it up before it runs

fallback:
this piece here plays a role
we will learn its part as we go
